message:
fix(service): resolve buffer waker race, add reconnect epoch tracking, and harden atomic writes

Buffer service had a subtle waker registration race: the caller's waker was
registered *after* polling the inner service, which meant a readiness edge
between poll_ready returning Pending and the waker registration could be lost
entirely, causing the caller to hang indefinitely. Fix by:
- Introducing InnerWakers (implements std::task::Wake) as a fanout waker type
- Registering the caller's waker *before* polling the inner service
- Polling the inner service with the fanout waker so all registered callers
  get notified on readiness transitions

Reconnect service now tracks a service_epoch (AtomicU64) and refresh_pending
(AtomicBool) to give callers visibility into connection lifecycle transitions.
disconnect() and failed reconnection attempts both increment the epoch and
clear the inner service through a unified invalidate_inner() path.

write_atomic_blocking now retries in a loop when the temp file name already
exists (stale temp from a previous crash), instead of failing with AlreadyExists.
The TempPathGuard is created after the file is successfully opened, preventing
cleanup of a file we didn't create.

NatsReadBuffer::consume() now clamps the position to buf.len() to prevent
silent buffer overrun when an oversized consume request is issued, keeping the
buffer usable for subsequent operations.

Also includes expanded notify_bug regression test coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
